name: Dar-us-Sakina
mission: DAR-US-SAKINA IS A 501(C)(3) NONPROFIT ORGANIZATION DEDICATED TO INCLUSION OF INDIVIDUALS WITH DISABILITIES IN THE MUSLIM COMMUNITY OF HOUSTON. OUR PLATFORM IS A SAFE SPACE FOR PEOPLE OF ALL ABILITIES AND THEIR CAREGIVERS. WE INSPIRE THE COMMUNITY TO ABANDON STIGMAS AND EMBRACE INDIVIDUALS WITH DISABILITIES WITH EMPATHY AND RESPECT. TOGETHER, WE ARE BETTER.
scoreSummary: Too early to rate numerically. This organization was founded in 2021 and is shown as an emerging organization while it builds a public track record.

evaluationSources: Support for Community Awareness Programs (Islamic Relief USA)

zakatClaimEvidence: Dar-us-Sakina explicitly states: "A large proportion of our programming is Zakat-eligible, allowing your donation to directly uplift individuals and families facing ongoing financial hardship due to disability." They also mention reserving "its Zakat fund for Muslim families who are in financial need or crisis." Furthermore, they have a "Zakat Program" and "Zakat Fund" to provide comprehensive support for special needs families in crisis, including emergency respite care, counseling, and durable medical equipment. On donation platforms, 'Zakat' is listed as a campaign option. (Source: https://www.every.org/dar-us-sakina)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Early-stage organization. We show qualitative context while it builds a longer public track record.

all_citations.claim: The organization holds a Charity Navigator score of 93.0/100 and a program expense ratio of 72.2%.
Total revenue for the period was $1,115,693.
The mission focuses on providing inclusive opportunities for individuals with disabilities in Houston.
The organization works to empower individuals with disabilities through community involvement.
The charity addresses specific religious and cultural needs within the Muslim community.
The organization operates specialized programs such as the Sakina Sunday School.
The organization supports families through a Zakat Fund and provides inclusive educational support hours.
Volunteer programs are used to engage the community and support organizational goals.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score due to its strong financial health and niche religious alignment, though its impact score is tempered by a focus on capacity building rather than direct cost-per-beneficiary metrics.
areas_for_improvement: Limited third-party impact data regarding long-term beneficiary outcomes
Opportunity to increase the program expense ratio to further direct more revenue toward service delivery

dimension_explanations.alignment: Dar-us-Sakina shows high alignment for Muslim donors by addressing the urgent and often underserved need for disability inclusion within a religious context [7][11]. Its zakat-eligible status and focus on community-specific services provide a strong fit for donors seeking religious and cultural impact [2][9].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates a commitment to capacity building through its Sakina Sunday School and Internship programs [8][9]. While it maintains a healthy program expense ratio of 72.2% and total revenue of $1,115,693, the lack of specific cost-per-beneficiary data results in a medium impact rating [1][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: Dar-us-Sakina provides inclusive educational and social opportunities for individuals with disabilities within the Houston Muslim community.

Strong fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.06 to raise every $1
Robust liquidity with 15.9 months of working capital available

The organization is recognized as zakat-eligible, allowing donors to fulfill their religious obligations while supporting inclusive services [2][9].

strengths.detail: The charity maintains a <cite id="1">93/100 overall rating from Charity Navigator</cite>, driven by a <cite id="1">72.2% independent board</cite> and high transparency standards.
Dar-us-Sakina spends only <cite id="1">$0.13 to raise every $1</cite>, ensuring that the vast majority of donor contributions are available for program services.
The organization holds <cite id="1">15.9 months of working capital</cite>, providing a massive safety net to sustain programs during economic downturns.

strengths_deep_dive: Dar-us-Sakina demonstrates a high level of fiscal responsibility that is rare for mid-sized community organizations. Their <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.06 per $1 raised</cite> is significantly better than many national peers. Furthermore, the organization has achieved a <cite id="3">3-year revenue CAGR of 24.6%</cite>, moving from $348,321 in 2021 to <cite id="3">$540,354 in 2023</cite>. This financial trajectory is supported by a <cite id="1">perfect 100 score in Culture & Community</cite> from Charity Navigator, suggesting strong local donor trust and engagement.
rich_narrative.summary: Dar-us-Sakina addresses a critical gap in the Houston Muslim community by providing inclusive educational and social opportunities for individuals with disabilities. The organization operates with a mission to empower this underserved population through specialized programs like the <cite id="8">Adult Day Program and Sakina Sunday School</cite>. Financial data from 2023 shows the charity managed <cite id="3">$540,354 in total revenue</cite>, representing a significant <cite id="3">3-year revenue growth rate of 24.6%</cite>. This growth allows the organization to maintain <cite id="9">two active masjid sites</cite> for its operations. While the charity is relatively young, it has established a strong institutional presence, earning a <cite id="1">93/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>. The organization is <cite id="2">verified as zakat-eligible</cite>, specifically serving the <cite id="2">fuqara (poor) and masakin (needy)</cite> within the disability community. By focusing on capacity building and direct service, Dar-us-Sakina ensures that families who often feel marginalized in religious spaces have access to <cite id="9">inclusive educational support hours</cite> and specialized caregiver training.
